title: PostgreSQL Proof of Concept Migration
description:
Validate the feasibility and effort required to migrate from MySQL/MariaDB to PostgreSQL. The goal is to confirm that the application can run on PostgreSQL without issues, but not to roll this change into production yet — the production migration would wait until the move to Forge.

The POC was completed successfully on a local machine. The application ran correctly on PostgreSQL with only connection configuration changes needed (updating the `.env` file).
acceptance_criteria: Application starts and connects to a PostgreSQL database without errors
All existing queries execute correctly against PostgreSQL
Migration requires only .env connection string changes, no application code rewrites
POC is validated on a local development environment
data_model_changes: Database engine swap from MySQL/MariaDB to PostgreSQL. Schema and queries validated for compatibility. Connection configuration updated in environment variables.
implementation_notes: Tested successfully on local machine. Production migration deferred to Forge transition. Only .env connection info changes required — no application code modifications needed.
comment_summary: Waqar confirmed the POC worked on his local machine with PostgreSQL and that production deployment would only require updating the .env connection information.
